sanskrit:
त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत्।
मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम्॥
transliteration: tribhir guṇa-mayair bhāvair ebhiḥ sarvam idaṁ jagat | mohitaṁ nābhijānāti mām ebhyo param avyayam ||
literal_meaning: By the three guṇa-made states-of-mind, all this world is bewildered; it does not know Me, who am beyond them and inexhaustible.
contextual_meaning: The psyche is hypnotized by its own conditioning (sattva, rajas, tamas), mistaking the colored lens for reality and remaining unaware of the unbroken awareness that witnesses every mood.
psychological_state: Trance-like identification with mental contents; dissociation from the observing Self.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media for hours, believing each post defines reality, then suddenly realizing you've lost touch with your core values.
anchor_text: Notice the mind's moods without buying their story.
